run.window_expansion: Strict 72h window (May 8–11) yielded 1 confirmed item. Expanded to 5 days (May 6–11) per skill rules.

items.title: Coder launches self-hosted AI coding agent platform for enterprise development teams

claims.claim: Coder Agents entered beta on May 6, 2026
Self-hosted architecture; no source code or prompts leave customer network
Model-agnostic: supports Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock, and local models
Free beta with no usage-based limits through September 2026
61% of engineering teams already running agents — vendor-cited market statistic; methodology not disclosed in press release

caveats: Beta-stage; full pricing post-September 2026 not disclosed
Self-hosted claims should be independently verified before recommending for regulated or air-gapped environments

items.title: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite reaches general availability with sub-dollar-per-million-token pricing

claims.claim: GA date: May 8, 2026
Pricing: $0.25/1M input tokens, $1.50/1M output tokens
P95 latency sub-second for classifier and tool-call workloads
claims.source: Gladly customer case study cited in Google Cloud blog
claims.caveat: Single customer deployment; not independently reproduced

claims.claim: ~60% lower cost than comparable thinking-tier models
claims.source: Gladly customer case study cited in Google Cloud blog

claims.claim: 2.5x faster response times and 45% faster output generation
claims.caveat: Vendor-claimed; no independent reproduction found during this run

claims.claim: Intelligence Index score: 34 vs 21 average for comparable models
FACTS factuality score: 40.6% vs 50.4% for Gemini 3.0 Flash Dynamic

caveats: Latency/cost figures from single-customer case study (Gladly), not a general benchmark
2.5x speed improvement is vendor-claimed without disclosed baseline or independent reproduction
FACTS factuality score 40.6% lags Gemini 3.0 Flash Dynamic (50.4%) — trade-off vs speed/cost

items.title: OpenAI Codex 0.130.0 adds headless remote-control server mode and AWS Bedrock authentication

claims.claim: codex remote-control headless entrypoint added
Bedrock authentication via AWS console-login credentials
Thread pagination with unloaded/summary/full turn item views
Live config refresh without server restart
view_image resolves files through multi-environment selectors

cross_references: https://releasebot.io/updates/openai/codex (T3 — date confirmation; GitHub fetch returned cached non-2026 date)
caveats: Date confirmed as May 9, 2026 via releasebot.io (T3); GitHub release tag page returned cached date metadata incompatible with 2026
Codex CLI (agentic coding tool) — distinct from the deprecated Codex API model

items.title: Anthropic transfers Petri alignment auditing tool to independent nonprofit Meridian Labs

claims.claim: Petri donated to Meridian Labs, described as an AI evaluation nonprofit
Petri 3.0 released simultaneously; auditor model now configurable separately from target model
Dish component runs evaluations using model's actual system prompt and live deployment framework
Petri used in Anthropic's alignment assessment since Claude Sonnet 4.5
Petri joins Inspect and Scout within Meridian Labs evaluation stack

cross_references: https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_petri/ (T3 — Meridian Labs documentation confirming tool integration)
caveats: Meridian Labs described as nonprofit; no independent governance documentation reviewed during this run
Organizational independence from Anthropic does not guarantee funding independence; relationship not fully disclosed in announcement

items.title: OpenAI publishes MRC networking protocol for AI supercomputers, deployed at 131,000+ GPU scale

claims.claim: MRC published May 6, 2026
Deployed at Oracle Cloud (Abilene, TX) and Microsoft Fairwater data center
131,000 GPU cluster topology achievable using two Ethernet switch tiers
Consortium partners: AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft, NVIDIA
claims.source: OpenAI primary blog; AMD blog; NVIDIA blog

claims.claim: Adaptive packet spraying eliminates core network congestion — vendor-claimed; no independent benchmark
claims.caveat: Vendor-claimed performance claim; no independent measurement available

caveats: Congestion-elimination claim is vendor-stated; no independent measurement reproduced during this run
Deployment at Oracle and Microsoft confirmed by OpenAI but not independently verified by those vendors in the sources accessed
